name: Kate Smith
abstract: Kathryn Elizabeth Smith (May 1, 1907 – June 17, 1986) was an American contralto. Referred to as The First Lady of Radio, Smith became well known for her renditions of "God Bless America" and "When the Moon Comes over the Mountain".

concerts.label: Benefit: U.S. Convalescent Hospital at Camp Upton, L.I.

works.t: When the Moon Comes Over the Mountain
Of Thee I Sing: Who Cares?
Right This Way: I'll Be Seeing You
Stop the World – I Want to Get Off: Act II. What Kind of Fool am I?

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: I liked Kate Smith better than anybody, you know. I thought her voice was so beautiful, you see. And I could actually sing just like her, you know.

about_quotes.speaker: Loren M. "Tex" Brewster
about_quotes.evidence: I used to. I can remember one night getting on the train. I've always remembered. I had. We had. We had our own private train. In case when I was with Kate and we had our own private car. We lived on the car you know. Slept on there. Thank you. All week. Mm-hmm.

about_quotes.speaker: Skitch Henderson
about_quotes.evidence: Skitch has been a pianist and musical director with many of America’s leading artists including Bing Crosby, Kate Smith and Frank Sinatra.
about_quotes.doc_title: Skitch Henderson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Milford, Connecticut, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: With Buddy Rich, I did a T.V. show with him, and we did shows with ... Kate Smith. Yeah we did a show with her.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Lewis interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Ruth & Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: I went back to New York and luckily I got a job as a staff musician playing for the “Kate Smith Show.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Ruth & Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Saddle River, New Jersey, January 19, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Wormworth
about_quotes.evidence: They used to do remotes on Kate Smith’s afternoon show... she’d feature Tyree periodically on her show
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Wormworth interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002
